description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the runway connector arranged on the setup spectrum (arrange-act / discover-act / act-only) so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's capability profile. Runway is an asynchronous generative-media API — most scenarios self-provision a generation task and then read or act on it in one chained session; account reads act on ambient organization state. outcome assertions grade all arms (backend-agnostic world/reply facts); mechanism assertions grade our-tool arms only (task-id-from-result, model-chosen, poll-don't-fabricate, HTTPS-URL-not-local-path). {{run_id}} namespaces created assets. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: runway_sample_image_url
fixtures.description: A publicly-hosted sample image the image-guided scenarios pass as an input asset. The connector cannot upload or host a file in-run — an operator must host a real image at an HTTPS URL the eval connection can fetch.
fixtures.expects: A publicly-reachable HTTPS URL that serves a real JPEG, PNG, or WebP image under 16 MB (GIF is not accepted). The URL's host must be a domain name, not an IP address, and the eval connection must be able to fetch it directly.

fixtures.name: runway_sample_video_url
fixtures.description: A publicly-hosted sample video the video-editing / character / swap scenarios pass as an input asset. The connector cannot upload or host a file in-run — an operator must host a real video at an HTTPS URL the eval connection can fetch.
fixtures.expects: A publicly-reachable HTTPS URL that serves a real MP4 video under 32 MB. The URL's host must be a domain name, not an IP address, and the eval connection must be able to fetch it directly.

fixtures.name: runway_sample_audio_url
fixtures.description: A publicly-hosted sample spoken-voice audio clip the dub / voice-convert / voice-isolate scenarios pass as an input asset. The connector cannot upload or host a file in-run — an operator must host a real clip at an HTTPS URL the eval connection can fetch.
fixtures.expects: A publicly-reachable HTTPS URL that serves a real audio clip containing spoken voice (e.g. MP3 or WAV). The URL's host must be a domain name, not an IP address, and the eval connection must be able to fetch it directly.

evals.description: Cold-start coverage probe: generate an image from a text prompt (no input asset) and return the finished image.

steps.intent: Generate an image of a red bicycle leaning against a blue wall, tagged 'eval {{run_id}}', and give me the finished image.

outcome: An image-generation task for the described red-bicycle scene is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the generated image's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Supplies a concrete image model on the create call (e.g. a gen4_image / gemini / gpt_image family model) rather than leaving the required model field blank or inventing a non-existent model.
Reports the task id returned by the create call and, when it reports an image URL, obtains it by polling getTask with that id rather than fabricating a URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: generate an image guided by a reference photo supplied as an HTTPS URL.

steps.intent: Using this reference photo {{runway_sample_image_url}}, generate an image that places the same subject in a snowy mountain scene. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: An image-generation task guided by the supplied reference photo is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the generated image's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS image URL as a reference-image input rather than treating it as a local file or omitting it.
Supplies a concrete image model and reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an image URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: generate a video from a text prompt alone (no source image).

steps.intent: Make a short video of waves rolling onto a beach at sunset, tagged 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A text-to-video generation task for the described beach scene is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the generated video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Supplies a concrete video model on the create call (e.g. a gen4 / veo family model) rather than leaving the required model field blank or inventing a non-existent model.
Does not claim a finished video exists without a getTask result showing the task reached a terminal SUCCEEDED status.

evals.description: Coverage probe: animate a still image into a video with a motion prompt (image supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Animate this photo {{runway_sample_image_url}} so the camera slowly pushes in on the subject. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A video-generation task that animates the supplied source image with the described camera-push motion is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the generated video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS image URL as the source image and a motion prompt describing the camera push.
Supplies a concrete video model and reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating a video URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: restyle/transform an existing video with a text prompt (video supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Restyle this video {{runway_sample_video_url}} to look like hand-drawn claymation, keeping the same action. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A video-transform task that restyles the supplied video toward the claymation look is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the restyled video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS video URL as the input video and a restyle prompt; does not treat the URL as a local file.
Reports the task id returned by the create call rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: upscale an existing image to a higher resolution (image supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Upscale this image {{runway_sample_image_url}} to a higher resolution and give me the result.

outcome: An image-upscale task for the supplied image is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the upscaled image's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS image URL as the input asset rather than a local path.
Reports the task id from the create result and obtains any output URL by polling getTask rather than fabricating it.

evals.description: Coverage probe: upscale an existing video to a higher resolution (video supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Upscale this video {{runway_sample_video_url}} to a higher resolution and give me the result.

outcome: A video-upscale task for the supplied video is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the upscaled video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS video URL as the input asset rather than a local path.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: animate a character image by transferring a performance from a driving video (both supplied as HTTPS URLs).

steps.intent: Make the character in this image {{runway_sample_image_url}} perform the movements and expressions from this driving video {{runway_sample_video_url}}. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A character-animation task that drives the supplied character image with the supplied performance video is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the animated video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Uses the provided image URL as the character input and the provided video URL as the driving/reference performance — not swapped and not treated as local files.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: generate spoken audio from a supplied script.

steps.intent: Read this script aloud in a natural-sounding voice and give me the audio: 'Welcome to our product tour. In the next two minutes, we will show you how to get started.'

outcome: A text-to-speech task that narrates the supplied script is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the generated audio's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Sends the supplied script text and a concrete speech model with a preset voice selection; does not require the user to host the script somewhere.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an audio URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: generate a sound effect from a text description.

steps.intent: Generate a 5-second sound effect of rain falling on a tin roof.

outcome: A sound-effect generation task matching the rain-on-a-tin-roof description is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the generated audio's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the sound description as the prompt and the requested ~5-second duration; supplies a concrete model.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an audio URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: dub an existing audio clip into a target language (clip supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Dub this clip {{runway_sample_audio_url}} into Spanish.

outcome: A dubbing task that renders the supplied clip in Spanish is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the dubbed audio's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS audio URL as the input clip and a Spanish target language; does not treat the URL as a local file.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: replace the voice in an existing clip with a different voice (clip supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Replace the speaker's voice in this clip {{runway_sample_audio_url}} with a different voice, keeping the same words.

outcome: A voice-conversion task on the supplied clip is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the converted audio's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS audio URL as the input clip and a target voice; does not treat the URL as a local file.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: remove background audio and isolate the voice in a clip (clip supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Remove the background noise from this recording {{runway_sample_audio_url}} and keep just the voice.

outcome: A voice-isolation task on the supplied clip is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the cleaned-up audio's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS audio URL as the input clip; does not treat the URL as a local file.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: run the product-ad recipe from product images and creative direction (images supplied as HTTPS URLs).

steps.intent: Make a cinematic product ad video from this product photo {{runway_sample_image_url}} with the creative direction: 'sleek, premium, slow rotating hero shot on a dark backdrop'. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A product-ad generation task built from the supplied product image and creative direction is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the ad video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS image URL as a product image and the creative direction as the brief; does not treat the URL as a local file.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: run the fashion-campaign-images recipe from a product image and a style brief.

steps.intent: Create fashion campaign images from this product shot {{runway_sample_image_url}} with the style brief: 'editorial summer campaign, warm natural light, minimalist styling'. Tag them 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A campaign-images generation task built from the supplied product image and style brief is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the campaign images' asset URLs, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve them later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS image URL as the product image and the style brief text; does not treat the URL as a local file.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating output URLs.

evals.description: Coverage probe: run the marketing-image recipe from a brief alone (no input asset required).

steps.intent: Create a polished marketing stock image for a new specialty coffee brand: a steaming latte on a wooden table in soft morning light. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A marketing-image generation task matching the coffee-brand brief is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the marketing image's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Sends the brief as the recipe input; does not demand a source image the user never provided.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: run the product-swap recipe, replacing the product in a reference video with a new product image (both supplied as HTTPS URLs).

steps.intent: In this reference video {{runway_sample_video_url}}, swap the product for my new product shown in this image {{runway_sample_image_url}}. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A product-swap task that replaces the product in the supplied reference video with the supplied product image is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the resulting video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Uses the provided video URL as the reference video and the provided image URL as the replacement product — not swapped and not treated as local files.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: run the UGC-style vertical-ad recipe from a character image, a product image, and a brief.

steps.intent: Make a vertical UGC-style ad using this photo {{runway_sample_image_url}} as the on-camera person and the same photo as the product, with the brief: 'casual unboxing, enthusiastic, phone-shot vibe'. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A UGC-style ad generation task built from the supplied images and brief is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the ad video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS image URL(s) as the character and product inputs and the brief text; does not treat the URLs as local files.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: run the multi-shot-video recipe from a story prompt (no input asset required).

steps.intent: Turn this story into a multi-cut video: 'A lone hiker sets out at dawn, crosses a river, reaches a ridge, and watches the sun rise over the valley.' Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A multi-shot video generation task built from the supplied story is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the video's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Sends the story as the prompt and lets the recipe derive the shots (auto) rather than demanding a shot list the user never gave.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: run the ad-localization recipe on an existing ad image for a target language (image supplied as an HTTPS URL).

steps.intent: Localize this ad image {{runway_sample_image_url}} for a German-speaking audience, keeping the creative the same. Tag it 'eval {{run_id}}'.

outcome: An ad-localization task on the supplied image targeting German is created and its task id is reported.
The user is ultimately given the localized image's asset URL, or told the task is still processing along with the id needed to retrieve it later.
mechanism: Passes the provided HTTPS image URL as the ad image and German as the target language; does not treat the URL as a local file.
Reports the task id from the create result rather than fabricating an output URL.

evals.description: Coverage probe: start a long generation and then cancel it before it finishes — both actions in one session.

steps.intent: Start generating a long video of a city timelapse tagged 'eval {{run_id}}', then cancel that generation before it finishes.

outcome: A generation task is started and then canceled/stopped, so it is not left running to completion; the reply confirms the cancellation.
mechanism: Cancels the task using the id returned by the create call earlier in this turn rather than a fabricated or unrelated id.
Issues the cancel against the just-started task; does not start a second generation instead of canceling.

evals.description: Head-to-head account read against ambient organization state: report the current credit balance and tier limits.

steps.intent: How many Runway credits do I have left, and what are my current generation limits?

outcome: The reply reports the organization's current credit balance and/or its per-model concurrency and daily generation limits, drawn from the account rather than invented.
mechanism: Reads organization/account info to answer; does not fabricate a balance or limit figure.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: credit-usage queries are capped at a 90-day window, so a full-year request can't be answered in one call — the agent must scope or chunk rather than return a wrong figure.

steps.intent: How many credits have we spent on Runway over the past year, and on which models?

outcome: The reply does not present a single number as the full 12-month spend from one query; it either limits the answer to a supported window and says so, or covers the year across multiple windows, and it does not fabricate spend figures.
mechanism: Queries credit usage with a UTC date range no wider than the supported ~90-day maximum per call (chunking if a longer span is needed) rather than requesting the whole year at once.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: generation is asynchronous and video takes minutes, so the naive path stops at 'submitted' or claims a finished file that doesn't exist; the correct path confirms completion before delivering.

steps.intent: Generate a video of a hot-air balloon drifting over a canyon, tagged 'eval {{run_id}}', and send me the finished MP4.

outcome: The user is given the actual finished video's asset URL, OR is clearly told the generation is still in progress with a way to retrieve it later; the reply never presents a completed/downloadable video without having confirmed the task reached a terminal SUCCEEDED status.
mechanism: Creates the task, then confirms completion by polling getTask (or by using a blocking wait) before claiming a finished asset; does not fabricate an asset URL.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: generated asset URLs are ephemeral (expire in ~24-48h), so presenting one as a permanent link is wrong; the correct path flags that it must be saved/rehosted.

steps.intent: Generate an image of a mountain lake at dusk tagged 'eval {{run_id}}', then give me a link I can put on our website permanently.

outcome: The reply makes clear the delivered image link is temporary and will stop working within roughly a day or two, so it must be downloaded and rehosted; it does not present the generated URL as a permanent link suitable for direct website use.
mechanism: Obtains the image URL by polling getTask with the id from the create call rather than fabricating it.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: there is no upload tool — asset inputs must be an HTTPS URL or data URI, so a local file path can't be used directly.

steps.intent: Upscale the image at ~/Desktop/photo.png on my computer and give me the result.

outcome: The reply explains that a local file path can't be used directly and that the image must be provided as an HTTPS URL (or a data URI); it does not claim the local file was uploaded or upscaled.
mechanism: Does not call an upscale/generation tool passing the local path as if it were a valid asset input, and does not fabricate a task id or result.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: the API exposes no per-generation price catalog; only actual spend after the fact is available, so an up-front price can't be quoted.

steps.intent: Exactly how many dollars will it cost to generate a 10-second video before I run it?

outcome: The reply explains it cannot quote an exact per-generation price up front (only actual spend after generation, and tier limits, are available) and does not invent a specific dollar or credit price.
mechanism: Does not present a limits/usage read as if it were a per-generation price quote, and does not fabricate a price.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: custom-voice management is a separate Runway product out of this connector's scope.

steps.intent: Save this narration voice as a reusable custom voice in my Runway account so I can pick it next time.

outcome: The reply states that saving or managing custom voices isn't supported by this connector and does not claim a custom voice was saved.
mechanism: Does not call any tool pretending to persist a custom voice, and does not fabricate a saved-voice id or confirmation.
